text: Git branching helps developers work together without causing conflicts. When starting a new task, you create a separate feature branch from the main codebase. This lets you try new ideas safely, as your changes stay isolated from the stable version. Once your work is ready, you request to merge it back into the main branch through a pull request. Other team members review your code and discuss improvements before confirming the merge. This whole process is called a version control workflow. It ensures that only tested and approved changes become part of the final project, reducing errors and keeping the history clean.
